payload.answer: Section headings ('Known facts', 'Interactions', 'Relationships') are organizational scaffolding and carry no factual weight. A groundedness check must skip them entirely and evaluate only the claims made in the body text beneath each heading. Treating a heading as a factual assertion would be a category error and would inflate or distort groundedness scores.
citations: Fixture ID: groundedness_section_headings_001 — Expected: headings ignored as factual claims.
payload.decision: Section headings are structural/organizational elements and must not be interpreted as factual assertions. Groundedness evaluation should focus solely on the substantive content beneath each heading, not on the headings themselves.

entity_resolution.reason: No specific named entity requires resolution; the fixture concerns evaluation methodology for section headings.

payload.intent: Evaluate whether section headings (Known facts, Interactions, Relationships) in an answer are incorrectly treated as factual claims when assessing groundedness.

memory_cards.statement: Section headings such as 'Known facts', 'Interactions', and 'Relationships' are presentational labels, not factual claims, and must be excluded from groundedness assessment.
Groundedness evaluation must target only the substantive statements within each section, not the organizational structure imposed by headings.

details: Section headings identified as non-factual structural elements.
Groundedness evaluation correctly scoped to substantive content only.
No memory cards committed for heading text as factual claims.

repair_options: Ensure the groundedness scorer explicitly filters out lines that match heading patterns (e.g., lines ending with no period, formatted as bold/title-case labels) before claim extraction.
Add a pre-processing step that strips structural metadata (headings, bullet markers, numbering) prior to factual claim enumeration.
Document in evaluation rubric that section headings are non-assertoric and must receive no groundedness score contribution.
